vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on passwd File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/passwd, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/passwd
vulnerability.group: Verify Permissions on Files with Local Account Information and Credentials

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting /etc/passwd permissions to 0644 is a standard STIG remediation with minimal risk of service disruption or access loss; it does not affect bootloader, partitioning, or critical runtime services.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: We need to follow workflow: first PLAN, then call review_plan function with plan description. So we need to output a plan description and then call review_plan.

review_feedback: QA output was not valid JSON. Raw: ...

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly sets /etc/passwd permissions to 0644, satisfying the STIG and OpenSCAP requirement.
suggested_improvements: Verify that the file ownership is root:root after permission change.
Consider using the symbolic mode (chmod u=rw,g=r,o=r) to explicitly clear any setuid/setgid bits, though 0644 already does this.
